---
title: "Stimuli"
subtitle: "`ImageStimulus`, `VideoStimulus`, `AudioStimulus`, `TextStimulus` - typed media inputs."
sidebar: concepts
---

## What it is

Stimulus wrappers are dataclasses that put media arrays or text behind a small
validation contract. They carry timing fields such as frame rate, onset,
duration, sample rate, or start offset before extraction begins. Every wrapper
also declares a `clock` and carries a `TemporalContext` for mappings to media,
experiment, run, or scan clocks.

## Invariants

- `ImageStimulus.image` is numeric and normalized to floating values clipped to `[0, 1]`.
- `VideoStimulus.frames` has a time axis first and a positive `fps`.
- `AudioStimulus.samples` is one- or two-dimensional and has a positive sample rate.
- `TextStimulus.text` is a Python string.
- `MultiModalStimulus` contains at least one non-empty modality.
- Clock identifiers are non-empty, and multimodal clock mappings must be mutually consistent.

## What it refuses

- Empty images, videos, or audio arrays.
- Invalid image, video, or audio ranks.
- Non-finite image onset values.
- Negative image duration values.
- Multi-modal inputs with no modalities.

## What it doesn't do

The wrappers do not decode arbitrary video containers by themselves. Path
coercion and media preparation live in workflow and preprocessing helpers. A
`start_offset_s` is a coordinate on the wrapper's declared clock; it is not an
implicit stimulus-to-scan mapping.

## See also

[Wrap a stimulus](../start/01-wrap-a-stimulus.qmd) · [Native time and clocks](native-time-and-clocks.qmd) · [Feature objects](feature-objects.qmd) · [Feature object schemas](../spec/feature-object-schemas.qmd)
